svg.text: Sediment Mn–Fe co-enrichment and water content as diagenetic indicators
Mn and Fe are redox-sensitive elements that co-enrich under reducing conditions. High water content in fine sediments promotes organic-matter preservation.
Mn–Fe co-enrichment under reducing conditions confirms diagenetic mobilization in fine depositional zones. Clay drives both Mn and Fe accumulation via surface sorption and redox trapping.
